seoTitle: East Ipswich vs East Mackay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between East Ipswich and East Mackay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: East Ipswich vs East Mackay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in East Ipswich and East Mackay.
heading: East Ipswich vs East Mackay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between East Ipswich, Queensland and East Mackay, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: East Mackay has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to East Ipswich (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is East Ipswich or East Mackay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: East Ipswich has a flood risk score of 47/100, while East Mackay scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: East Ipswich scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. East Mackay scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in East Ipswich or East Mackay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. East Mackay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
